target.notes: Primary nitrogen assimilation route under nitrogen limitation: glutamine synthetase (GS) fixes NH4+ onto L-glutamate; glutamate synthase (GOGAT) regenerates two L-glutamate from L-glutamine + 2-oxoglutarate with NADPH.

reactions.equation_hint: L-glutamate + NH3 + ATP -> L-glutamine + ADP + Pi (glutamine synthetase)
L-glutamine + 2-oxoglutarate + NADPH -> 2 L-glutamate + NADP+ (glutamate synthase, NADPH-dependent)

citations.note: Reitzer: nitrogen assimilation in Escherichia coli and Salmonella.
Vanoni & Curti: glutamate synthase structure and mechanism.

rationale: Under low ammonium, E. coli switches from glutamate dehydrogenase (GDH, high Km for NH4+) to the ATP-dependent GS/GOGAT cycle. GS (R1) uses ATP to condense NH4+ onto glutamate, producing glutamine; GOGAT (R2) splits glutamine with 2-oxoglutarate and NADPH, yielding two glutamates and regenerating the cycle. Net result: one 2-oxoglutarate + one NH4+ + one ATP + one NADPH -> one glutamate.
